assumptions: On every change in the input signal (IN) or positive edge of CLK or RST, if RST is active, the output signal MATCH is set to 0.

If the sequence of inputs IN is 1, 0, 0, 1, 1, the MATCH signal is 1 at the same time as the last occurrence of IN=1. If the sequence of inputs IN is 1, 0, 0, 1, 1, 0, 0, 1, 1, the MATCH signal becomes 1 at the fifth and ninth of IN; otherwise, it is set to 0.
Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.

expected_properties: Observed outputs or protocol-visible signals settle to a stable, non-X post-reset state.
No fixed-cycle completion is assumed during reset recovery.
cases.goal: Establish a stable post-reset baseline before functional checking.
notes: Reset polarity may still be heuristic if the contract marks it ambiguous.
preconditions: Reset signal is available in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Assert the detected reset using the inferred polarity.
Release reset conservatively and observe interface stabilization.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: MATCH becomes 1 exactly when the fifth input of a 1,0,0,1,1 pattern is observed, and returns to 0 otherwise.
cases.goal: Validate that the FSM asserts MATCH on the correct pattern occurrence in a continuous stream.
preconditions: Reset has been released and the FSM is in idle state.
stimulus_intent: Drive a stream of IN bits containing the pattern 1,0,0,1,1 and verify MATCH toggles at the correct cycle.
timing_assumptions: Observe MATCH after one clock cycle following the last pattern bit.

expected_properties: MATCH remains 0 for a long sequence of zeros.
cases.goal: Confirm that no false MATCH is generated for zero‑only input.
timing_assumptions: No MATCH asserted during zero stream.
